nger from fire; when with darkness, mental aberration will be
the result. If the figures of snakes fall or break, the minister will
come to grief. If portents are sighted in the north and other quar-
ters, Brahmanas and other classes in order will suffer. If any of the
minor standards, called Tndra's Daughters', breaks, courtezans will
be killed. Should the ropes snap, there would be trouble to children.
If the prop at the bottom breaks, the king's mother will be troubled.
Whatever good or bad is done by boys or actors (or wandering
ministrels) at the time will have corresponding effects on the
people.

After worshipping the erected Standard of Indra for four days,
the king should discard it on the fifth day (i.e. the first day of the
dark fortnight) after due worship in the company of his ministers
for the rise of his own power.
[The metre is ffafq*rf*5f?T].
